nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=90 beats='Pip the young kit fox stands in his sandy burrow den; tonight is the Desert Star Bazaar and there is still so much to gather; Pip counts on her paws what she needs and decides where to set out first; warm and excited, never worried'>>

choices.label: Go pick firefly beetles by the cactus grove.
Go invite your friends at the rock burrow.
Go cut yucca stalks for beacon frames.
Go ask the harvester ants for mesquite cakes.
Go fetch the old star banners.
Go ask the coyote trio to play.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='at the cactus grove the low firefly beetles are ripe and glowing softly, but a shinier cluster hangs high on a wobbly branch; Pip decides whether to fill her basket with the easy low ones or stretch up for the high ones'>>

choices.label: Pick the ripe low firefly beetles.
Climb for the high cluster.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='Pip fills her basket with glowing firefly beetles that light her chin from below; the gathering is beginning to grow; Pip can carry the firefly beetles onward to the canyon square or trot back to his sandy burrow den to fetch more things first'>>

choices.label: Carry the firefly beetles to the canyon square.
Go back to his sandy burrow den for more.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='under the rock burrow Pip finds her friends dozing in the warm afternoon; Pip wants to invite them all to the Desert Star Bazaar; Pip decides whether to knock gently and wake them softly or clang a booming drum to hurry things along'>>

choices.label: Knock gently on the rock burrow door.
Clang a booming drum.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='her friends yawn, smile, and say yes at once, grabbing their little beacons; the group is growing; Pip can lead the happy troop straight to the canyon square or swing back by his sandy burrow den to grab the next thing on her list'>>

choices.label: Lead your friends to the canyon square.
Pop back to his sandy burrow den first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='at the dry wash Pip needs stalks to bend into beacon frames; the tall dry yucca stalks rustle on the bank while fresh green ones lean out over the water; Pip decides which yucca stalks to cut'>>

choices.label: Cut the tall dry yucca stalks.
Cut the fresh green yucca stalks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='the dry yucca stalks snap free in a neat golden bundle, perfect for bending; Pip can start weaving the beacon frames right here by the dry wash or lug the whole bundle back to his sandy burrow den to work by her stump'>>

choices.label: Weave the frames here by the dry wash.
Carry the bundle back to his sandy burrow den.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='Pip bends the yucca stalks into round little beacon frames, humming as she works; a stack of them balances in her arms; there is only one thing to do now, carry the finished frames to the canyon square'>>

choices.label: Carry the beacon frames to the canyon square.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='at the mesquite thicket Pip asks the harvester ants for a few mesquite cakes for the festival feast; the harvester ants buzz kindly; Pip decides whether to wait politely for them to pack a basket or dash back to his sandy burrow den and come by for the cakes later'>>

choices.label: Wait politely for mesquite cakes.
Head back to his sandy burrow den for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='the harvester ants load a basket with warm golden mesquite cakes that smell of clover; the feast is coming together; Pip thanks them and carries the sweet-smelling basket straight to the canyon square'>>

choices.label: Carry the mesquite cakes to the canyon square.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='in the tool shed the old star banners from last year sit dusty on a shelf; Pip decides whether to dust each one off carefully now or scoot back to his sandy burrow den and fetch them on the next trip'>>

choices.label: Dust off the old star banners.
Go back to his sandy burrow den for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='the old star banners come up bright and pretty under Pip's little cloth, their painted patterns good as new; her armful of festival treasures keeps growing; Pip carries them off toward the canyon square'>>

choices.label: Carry the old star banners to the canyon square.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='by the wide dunes the coyote trio tunes their tiny fiddles; Pip asks if they will play at the Desert Star Bazaar; they chirp eagerly; Pip decides whether to lead them along right away or pop back to his sandy burrow den to gather one more thing first'>>

choices.label: Ask the coyote trio to follow you now.
Go back to his sandy burrow den first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='the coyote trio forms a cheerful little parade behind Pip, fiddles under their arms; music will make the night complete; Pip leads the chirping players all the way to the canyon square'>>

choices.label: Lead the coyote trio to the canyon square.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='the canyon square opens wide at dusk, ringed by the tall saguaro and the quiet spring; everything Pip has gathered is piling up and friends are arriving; Pip decides whether to start setting the festival up now or make one last trip back to his sandy burrow den for anything forgotten'>>

choices.label: Start setting up the festival.
Make one last trip to his sandy burrow den.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=85 beats='in the canyon square the beacon frames get wrapped in bright paper and the firefly beetles tucked inside to shimmer; friends bustle everywhere; Pip decides whether to hang the beacons up in the tall saguaro or spread out the picnic feast first'>>

choices.label: Hang the beacons in the tall saguaro.
Spread out the picnic feast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=85 beats='friends lift the finished beacons into the branches of the tall saguaro, where they sway like sleepy fireflies; the sky is turning purple; Pip decides whether to light them right this moment or wait for the very first star to appear'>>

choices.label: Light the beacons right now.
Wait for the first star.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=85 beats='one by one the beacons wake up with warm firefly beetles light and the canyon square glows entirely gold; everyone gasps with delight; Pip decides whether to set some beacons floating out across the quiet spring or keep them all glowing high in the tall saguaro'>>

choices.label: Float beacons on the quiet spring.
Keep the beacons in the tall saguaro.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='everyone grows hushed and watches the darkening sky until the first tiny star blinks awake over the canyon square; a happy cheer goes up; that is the signal, and Pip lights the very first beacon to begin the festival glow'>>

choices.label: Light the first beacon and begin.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=85 beats='beacon after beacon flickers on until the canyon square is a sea of soft golden light and the quiet spring mirrors it all; the coyote trio lift their fiddles; Pip decides whether to start the bazaar chant for everyone or gather her friends to make a wish together'>>

choices.label: Start the bazaar chant.
Make a wish together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='the coyote trio strike up a bouncy tune and Pip leads the first verse of the bazaar chant; friends join line by line until the canyon square rings with song, the tall saguaro glowing overhead'>>

choices.label: Sing the festival into the night.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='the friends gather in a ring around the brightest beacon, close their eyes, and hold paws; Pip counts softly to three so everyone can make the same warm wish for the woodland at once'>>

choices.label: Make the wish all together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=85 beats='Pip spreads a checked cloth on the grass and lays out mesquite cakes and berry treats while beacons glow softly overhead; friends settle around; Pip decides whether to start sharing mesquite cakes now or wait for old Uncle Badger to arrive and bless the feast'>>

choices.label: Start sharing mesquite cakes.
Wait for Uncle Badger.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='mesquite cakes go round the glowing circle and everyone munches happily under the swaying beacons; the feast is a merry success; Pip decides whether to let the feast roll on into the night or start a round of giggly a shadow-guesss between bites'>>

choices.label: Let the happy feast roll on.
Start a round of a shadow-guesss.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=75 beats='between bites of mesquite cakes the friends trade silly a shadow-guesss, each one sillier than the last, until everyone is laughing so hard the beacon light seems to giggle too; Pip saves the very best one for the end'>>

choices.label: Tell the very best a shadow-guess.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=80 beats='Uncle Badger arrives, wrapped in a woven blanket, whiskers dusted with sand; the friends make room and grow quiet; Uncle Badger smiles and begins the legend of the first bazaar star, just as she does every year'>>

choices.label: Listen to Uncle Badger's beacon story.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=75 beats='Pip stretches for a shinier cluster, the wobbly branch springs back, and her whole basket of firefly beetles bounces away into a heap of soft sand with a comic boing; nobody is hurt and Pip laughs, dusting off to trot back to his sandy burrow den and start her gathering again'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=75 beats='a booming drum clangs and every sleepy friend tumbles out of the rock burrow at once in a giggly, grumbly heap, blankets over their heads; it takes ages to sort out the muddle, so Pip decides to start over and invite them the gentle way next time'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=75 beats='the fresh green yucca stalks are far too soft and wet to hold a shape; every frame Pip bends just flops over like cooked noodles and drips on her paws; Pip laughs at the soggy mess and heads back to his sandy burrow den to begin again with the right yucca stalks'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='Pip and her friends set glowing beacons adrift on the quiet spring, where they float like little golden moons doubled in the water; everyone in the canyon square ohhs together; the festival is beautiful and complete, and Pip glows as bright as her beacons'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='the tall saguaro blazes with hanging beacons like a tree full of warm stars, and everyone dances slowly beneath its golden branches; Pip looks up at all she and her friends have made together, her heart as full and bright as the festival glow'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='the canyon square rings with the bazaar chant, the tall saguaro glowing above and the coyote trio fiddles sailing over every voice; the music rolls out over the quiet spring and into the night; Pip sings loudest of all, proud of the festival her gathering has grown into'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='in the ring of beacon light the friends make one warm wish together for a happy woodland year, then open their eyes to the canyon square glowing all around them; Pip squeezes her friends' paws, sure that this is the best festival there ever was'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='mesquite cakes and berry treats go round and round beneath the swaying beacons while everyone laughs and nibbles late into the glowing night; the feast Pip gathered piece by piece has brought the whole woodland together, warm and merry'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='Pip tells the very best a shadow-guess of all and the whole glowing circle roars with laughter, mesquite cakes nearly forgotten; the beacons seem to twinkle along with the giggling; it is the merriest festival night the woodland friends can remember'>>

ending.title: The Best Guess of the Night

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=85 beats='Uncle Badger finishes the legend of the first bazaar star just as every beacon in the canyon square glows its brightest, and the friends clap softly; Pip leans against Uncle Badger, happy that her gathering has kept the gentle festival alive one more year'>>
